title: Sanitize Your CPR Pocket Mask
description: Clean and disinfect your CPR mask so it's safe for the next emergency.

dialogue.text: Your CPR pocket mask hasn't been cleaned since the last practice session. Let's sanitize it so it's ready for the next use.
Operational safety: wear gloves and mix disinfectant correctly. If the mask has cracks or a torn valve, retire it and replace instead of reusing it.

options.text: Mask body is intact; continue sanitizing.
Mask is damaged; remove it from service.

dialogue.text: Troubleshooting branch: replace the damaged mask before the next CPR drill. Do not store compromised gear in your response kit.

options.text: Replacement mask ready; resume sanitizing workflow.

dialogue.text: Wash with warm soapy water, rinse thoroughly, then sanitize using your first aid protocol.

options.text: Sanitizing complete and contamination controls followed.
I skipped contact time or contamination happened; redo sanitizing.

dialogue.text: Recovery loop: rewash the mask and repeat disinfectant contact time from the start before storage.
Evidence gate: confirm the mask is fully air-dried and sealed in the kit to avoid bacterial growth.

options.text: Mask is sanitized, dry, and staged in the first aid kit.
Mask is still damp or storage wasn't sterile; repeat sanitizing.

dialogue.text: All clean! A sanitized mask protects both you and your patient during resuscitation.
